name: Pawn1st
description_short: Pawn1st Mesa, Arizona — FirstCash is the leading international operator of pawn stores with 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, the UK, and Latin Am...
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the largest pawn shop chain internationally. Founded with a focus on serving cash and credit-constrained consumers, the company has built a massive retail footprint spanning over 3,300 pawn store locations. Beyond pawn operations, FirstCash also owns AFF, a subsidiary providing lease-to-own and retail finance solutions through 15,000+ merchant partner locations nationwide. The company employs approximately 22,000 people and is listed in both the S&P MidCap 400 Index and Russell 2000 Index.

FirstCash's core services revolve around non-recourse pawn loans secured by pledged personal property. Customers can obtain fast cash by using items of value as collateral—jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments. The company also buys and sells merchandise directly, runs a gold/precious metals buying service, and offers layaway plans requiring 10% down payments. Their retail locations function as both lending facilities and consignment/resale shops with rotating inventory.

The company distinguishes itself through scale and geographic reach. With over 3,300 stores across North America and Latin America, FirstCash offers convenience and accessibility that smaller pawn operators cannot match. Their inclusion in major stock indices reflects institutional recognition as a stable, legitimate financial services provider. The company also expanded into retail finance through the AFF subsidiary, diversifying revenue beyond traditional pawn lending.
  
Honestly assessed, FirstCash serves a legitimate market need for people who cannot access traditional credit. Pawn loans are non-recourse, meaning customers don't risk debt collection if they cannot reclaim items. However, pawn lending involves implicit high costs—you forfeit the pledged item's full value if you don't repay, which functions economically like a high-interest loan. The retail merchandise side means inventory is unpredictable and prices may not reflect fair market value. For consumers with no credit access, this is a viable option; for others, it should be a last resort.
pros: Non-recourse pawn loans—no debt collection if you cannot repay; you simply lose the collateral
Same-day cash availability; no credit check required
Massive network of 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, UK, and Latin America for convenience
Buys gold, jewelry, and precious metals at fair market rates with immediate cash payment
Layaway service available with only 10% down, providing alternative payment flexibility
Publicly traded company (S&P MidCap 400, Russell 2000) providing transparency and regulatory oversight
Wide range of merchandise acceptance—jewelry, electronics, tools, instruments, and more
cons: High implicit cost: losing collateral value functions as an expensive loan; reclaiming pawned items requires repaying principal plus interest charges
Inventory unpredictability—store stock rotates and specific items may not be available
Retail merchandise pricing may not reflect fair market value or competitive retail pricing
Limited to customers with valuable personal property to pledge; not accessible to those without collateral
Pawn loans are short-term solutions only; not suitable for addressing underlying financial instability
best_for: People with no credit history or poor credit who need emergency cash and have valuable items to pledge
Individuals who need fast, same-day cash without undergoing credit checks or lengthy approval processes
Consumers seeking to buy used merchandise at discounted prices through retail inventory
People who want to sell gold, jewelry, or other precious metals for immediate cash
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for unbanked or credit-constrained consumers who own valuable items and need same-day emergency cash without credit checks. The main caveat is that pawn lending carries high implicit costs—you lose collateral value that typically exceeds the amount borrowed, making it economically expensive compared to traditional loans, and it solves only immediate cash needs, not underlying financial problems.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, musical instruments)
Gold and precious metals buying service with immediate cash payment
Retail sales of used merchandise from pawn inventory
Layaway plans with 10% down payment option
Lease-to-own payment solutions through AFF subsidiary (15,000+ merchant partners)
Retail finance payment solutions for consumer goods
Same-day cash advances on pledged collateral
